title: Heart o' the North
content:
And when I come to the dim trail-end,
        I who have been Life's rover,
        This is all I would ask, my friend,
        Over and over and over:

        A little space on a stony hill
        With never another near me,
        Sky o' the North that's vast and still,
        With a single star to cheer me;

        Star that gleams on a moss-grey stone
        Graven by those who love me -
        There would I lie alone, alone,
        With a single pine above me;

        Pine that the north wind whinneys through -
        Oh, I have been Life's lover!
        But there I'd lie and listen to
        Eternity passing over.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert William Service, titled "Heart o' the North", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
